purpose: Fresh DOM-only LLM playthroughs to catch every issue Steam reviewers would hit

outcomes: DRIVEN BACK — died Floor 1 vs Staple Bug (0 damage dealt, Quick Play blocked)
DRIVEN BACK — died Floor 1 vs Bookmark Vine (16→0 HP)
DRIVEN BACK — died Floor 1 vs Thesis Construct (9→0 HP, enemy at 12 HP)

issues.title: Charge Play key (C) does not trigger quiz — silently executes as Quick Play
issues.description: After selecting a card with key '1', pressing 'C' decremented AP and registered a card play (cardsPlayedThisTurn: 0→1) but no quiz overlay appeared. The core learning mechanic — answering a question for bonus damage — is non-functional via the keyboard interface.
issues.steamReviewerQuote: Charge Play is supposed to let me answer questions for bonus damage, but pressing C just plays the card normally.
issues.workaround: None found — Phaser canvas hover may still work but couldn't be verified via DOM

issues.title: DOM .click() on card-hand-N buttons does not register card plays in game engine
issues.description: Clicking card-hand-N DOM buttons triggers CSS animation (card-playing class) but cardsPlayedThisTurn stays 0 and AP doesn't change. Mouse players must click in the Phaser canvas area, not the DOM button overlay. Keyboard 1-5 keys DO register correctly.
issues.steamReviewerQuote: Clicking the cards does nothing. I had to figure out there's a keyboard shortcut just to play cards.
issues.workaround: Use keyboard keys 1-5 to play cards

issues.title: Dungeon map initial scroll shows boss area — starting nodes below viewport
issues.description: Map container (scrollHeight 1398, clientHeight 1006) loads at scrollTop=108, showing near the top/boss. First available nodes (row 0) sit at y≈1233 — below the 1080px viewport. Player sees a near-black screen with zero clickable elements.
issues.steamReviewerQuote: I can't click anything after starting a run. The map is empty.

issues.title: Charge Play mechanic completely undiscoverable via normal click interaction
issues.description: Clicking a card immediately Quick-Plays it with no quiz. Charge Play requires hovering in the Phaser canvas to see a card popover showing Quick/Charge options. No persistent DOM UI affordance exists. Against block-heavy enemies, Quick Play deals 0 net damage.
issues.steamReviewerQuote: I can't damage the enemy. Cards don't work.
issues.workaround: Use '?' help button to discover keyboard shortcuts, then use Q/C keys

issues.title: Charge Play discovery requires finding ? button or hovering Phaser canvas — no persistent hint
issues.description: The only ways to discover Quick vs Charge Play: (a) find and click the non-obvious '?' button to show keyboard help overlay, or (b) hover over a card to see Phaser canvas popover. No persistent UI element shows this mechanic.
issues.steamReviewerQuote: I went 5 turns without knowing I could answer questions for full damage.
issues.workaround: Click ? button in bottom-right corner of combat screen

issues.title: First combat tutorial requires 5 sequential dismiss clicks
issues.description: 5 sequential 'Got it' tutorial popups appeared on first combat encounter before being able to play any card. Each requires a separate click.
issues.steamReviewerQuote: Too many tutorial popups before I can start playing.

issues.title: 'You still have AP' end-turn confirmation dialog fires every turn
issues.description: Every turn where AP remains, pressing End Turn triggers a confirmation dialog that requires a second click. This doubles the required interaction for every turn and fires even when the player is at critically low HP.
issues.steamReviewerQuote: Annoying popup every single turn asking me to confirm.

issues.title: ENCOUNTERS counter shows 0 on runEnd despite completing a combat encounter
issues.description: The ENCOUNTERS stat on the runEnd screen shows 0 across all 3 runs, despite each run completing 1 combat encounter (entering combat, playing cards, dying).
issues.steamReviewerQuote: It says 0 encounters but I just fought a monster.

issues.title: 'Practice Run' penalty fires for new players who never answered a knowledge question
issues.description: 100% accuracy (from using only Quick Play with no wrong answers possible) triggers 'Practice Run — No camp rewards — you already know this material.' A new player who hasn't answered a single quiz question sees this banner, which is contextually wrong and confusing.
issues.steamReviewerQuote: The game told me I already know everything but I haven't answered any questions yet.

issues.title: '0% of dungeon explored' shown after dying deep in Floor 1 combat
issues.description: After fighting 6+ minutes and 10+ turns of combat on Floor 1, runEnd shows 'you only explored 0% of the dungeon.' The exploration percentage never increases from 0%.
issues.steamReviewerQuote: I fought for 6 minutes and it says I explored 0% of the dungeon.

issues.title: No shop, mystery, or rest room types visible on any map across all 3 runs
issues.description: All 3 dungeon maps showed 100% combat nodes (16 combat + 1 elite + 1 boss). No shop, mystery event, or rest rooms appeared in any run. This limits roguelite variety and makes the game feel like a linear combat gauntlet.
issues.steamReviewerQuote: There's no shop or rest areas, just constant combat. The map feels shallow.
issues.workaround: None — may require progressing further into the dungeon

issues.title: Narration dismiss button not clickable by text content — requires overlay click or auto-dismiss
issues.description: The narration dismiss button has no textContent (text rendered via CSS :before or child elements), so DOM text-based queries fail. Clicking the overlay div itself works. Real players with a mouse would click the overlay naturally.
issues.steamReviewerQuote: Non-issue for real player with mouse.

issues.title: rrScreenshot during runEnd captures Phaser canvas background, not Svelte overlay
issues.description: When screen=runEnd, rrScreenshot shows the Phaser combat background (enemy sprite visible) rather than the runEnd Svelte component overlay. The overlay is DOM-only and sits above the canvas. Functional on real hardware, but the visual bleed should be verified — runEnd overlay must fully cover the combat canvas.
issues.steamReviewerQuote: Possible visual layering issue — combat background visible under runEnd screen.
issues.workaround: DOM inspection confirms runEnd data is correct; visual is a screenshot capture issue

prioritizedFixes.reason: Blocks the entire game on first launch. Every new player hits this on every run.
Card clicks don't register — the most fundamental game interaction is broken for mouse-only players.
Charge Play is the core learning mechanic and it silently fails. Players can't access the primary feedback loop.
No discoverability for Charge Play. Combined with ISSUE-3-1, the knowledge quiz may never be reached.
ENCOUNTERS: 0 is a visible data bug on the summary screen that directly undermines player confidence in the stats.

positives: Hub screen is beautiful and immediately intuitive — 'click the door to start' is clear.
Deck selection (Trivia Dungeon vs Study Temple) is well-labeled and clean.
Enemy visual variety is excellent across 3 runs: Staple Bug, Bookmark Vine, Thesis Construct.
Combat backgrounds are atmospheric and high quality (industrial pipes, vine library, crystal cave).
Thesis Construct's escalating Crystal Slam creates genuine tactical pressure — good design.
Knowledge Harvest breakdown (SEEN/REVIEWING/MASTERED) is genuinely compelling end-screen data.
Death flavor text is excellent: 'Remind yourself that overconfidence is a slow and insidious killer.'
Keyboard shortcut system (1-5, Enter) works reliably once discovered.
Total XP reward (+28 for 10 knowledge items) feels generous and motivating.
All 3 runs completed full loop: hub → combat → runEnd → hub with no softlocks or crashes.
